title: Three Heads, One Number Lesson
summary: A Cerberus with three curious heads learns to count from one to ten and use number words in daily tasks.

story_text.male: {{pet_name}} had three heads, and each head shouted a different number. He took a deep breath and said, 'We will count together.' He walked up the hill and counted each step from one to five.
story_text.female: {{pet_name}} had three heads, and each head shouted a different number. She took a deep breath and said, 'We will count together.' She walked up the hill and counted each step from one to five.
story_text.neutral: {{pet_name}} had three heads, and each head shouted a different number. {{pet_name}} took a deep breath and said, 'We will count together.' {{pet_name}} walked up the hill and counted each step from one to five.

speech_bubble_lines.male: Let us count together: one, two, three, four, five.
speech_bubble_lines.female: Let us count together: one, two, three, four, five.
speech_bubble_lines.neutral: Let us count together: one, two, three, four, five.

text.neutral: {{pet_name}} counts steps from one to five.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct counting order from one to five.

episodes.title: Count to Ten at the Gate

story_text.male: At the city gate, {{pet_name}} helped sort ten small stones. He placed them in two rows and counted out loud: one, two, three, four, five, six, seven, eight, nine, ten. All three heads smiled because they agreed.
story_text.female: At the city gate, {{pet_name}} helped sort ten small stones. She placed them in two rows and counted out loud: one, two, three, four, five, six, seven, eight, nine, ten. All three heads smiled because they agreed.
story_text.neutral: At the city gate, {{pet_name}} helped sort ten small stones. {{pet_name}} placed them in two rows and counted out loud: one, two, three, four, five, six, seven, eight, nine, ten. All three heads smiled because they agreed.

text.neutral: The three heads agreed on the numbers.

prompt.male: He sorted ten ____ at the gate.
prompt.female: She sorted ten ____ at the gate.

questions.prompt: Complete the sequence: six, seven, eight, ____, ____.
